Allergic Rhinitis.comments: Adjunct therapy; consider in asthma comorbidity

Anaphylaxis.dose: 0.3 mg IM into thigh; repeat every 5–15 min if needed

Anaphylaxis.comments: Adjunct to epinephrine for cutaneous symptoms

Angioedema.dose: 0.3 mg IM for severe swelling or airway compromise
Angioedema.comments: Use immediately if tongue or throat swelling

Food Allergy.comments: For mild reactions such as itching or urticaria

Food Allergy.dose: 0.3 mg IM in thigh at first sign of anaphylaxis; may repeat after 5–15 min
Food Allergy.comments: Carry at all times; lifesaving for severe reactions

Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.dose: 25–50 mg orally twice daily; titrate to HR <100 bpm
Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.comments: Preferred for rate control in AF

Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.dose: 120–360 mg/day in divided doses or extended release
Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.comments: Alternative rate control agent; avoid in heart failure
Useful in sedentary patients; monitor levels

Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.dose: Load 800–1,600 mg/day until arrhythmia controlled then 200–400 mg/day maintenance
Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.comments: Use when rate control fails or rhythm control desired; multiple adverse effects

Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.dose: Apixaban 5 mg BID (reduce to 2.5 mg BID if ≥80 years, ≤60 kg or creatinine ≥133 μmol/L); Rivaroxaban 20 mg once daily (15 mg with CrCl 15–49 mL/min); Dabigatran 150 mg BID (110 mg BID for age ≥80 or high bleeding risk); Edoxaban 60 mg once daily (30 mg once daily if CrCl 15–50 mL/min)
Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.comments: Preferred anticoagulation for non‑valvular AF with CHADS‑65/CHADS‑VASc stroke risk; evaluate renal function and bleeding risk; do not use in mechanical valves or moderate–severe mitral stenosis

Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.dose: Adjust dose to maintain INR 2–3; start 1–5 mg orally daily and titrate based on INR with frequent monitoring; overlap with heparin in some cases
Atrial Fibrillation/Flutter.comments: Use when DOAC contraindicated (mechanical heart valve, moderate/severe mitral stenosis, severe renal impairment, cost issues); requires regular INR monitoring and dietary counselling

Chest Pain (initial).dose: 0.3–0.6 mg sublingually every 5 minutes up to 3 doses
Chest Pain (initial).comments: Rapid relief of ischemic chest pain
Reduces mortality in suspected acute coronary syndrome

Chest Pain (initial).dose: 5 mg IV every 5 minutes up to 15 mg then 25–50 mg orally every 6 hours
Chest Pain (initial).comments: Use if hypertensive/tachycardic; avoid in acute heart failure

Dyslipidemia.dose: Start 10–20 mg once daily; maintenance 10–80 mg/day
Dyslipidemia.comments: Cornerstone therapy; titrate to LDL target
Add if statin not achieving goal or not tolerated

Dyslipidemia.dose: 75 mg subcutaneous every 2 weeks; may increase to 150 mg
Dyslipidemia.comments: Use in very high risk patients when LDL remains high

Dyslipidemia.dose: 4 g orally once or twice daily, titrating to 12–16 g/day
Dyslipidemia.comments: Adjunct when statins and ezetimibe inadequate

Edema.dose: 20–80 mg orally once daily, titrate per response
Edema.comments: Relieves volume overload in HF and renal disease

Edema.dose: 25 mg once daily; up to 50 mg/day
Edema.comments: May potentiate loop diuretics in refractory edema
Useful in cirrhotic ascites; monitor potassium

Heart Failure.dose: Start 2.5 mg daily; titrate to 5–10 mg twice daily
Heart Failure.comments: Foundation therapy in HFrEF; monitor renal function

Heart Failure.dose: Start 12.5–25 mg once daily; titrate to 200 mg/day
Heart Failure.comments: Use evidence-based beta blocker; start low and go slow

Heart Failure.dose: 49/51 mg orally twice daily; titrate to 97/103 mg BID
Heart Failure.comments: Replace ACE/ARB when tolerated; requires washout of ACE inhibitor

Heart Failure.dose: 12.5–25 mg orally once daily; up to 50 mg/day
Heart Failure.comments: Add in symptomatic HFrEF; monitor potassium and creatinine

Heart Failure.dose: 20–80 mg orally once daily; titrate per volume status
Heart Failure.comments: For fluid overload; adjust dose to achieve euvolemia

Hypertension.dose: Start 2.5 mg orally daily; titrate to 2.5–20 mg daily in 1–2 doses
Hypertension.comments: Preferred in diabetes/CKD; monitor creatinine & potassium

Hypertension.dose: Start 50 mg orally daily; titrate to 50–100 mg/day
Hypertension.comments: Use if ACE‑i intolerant; monitor kidney function & potassium

Hypertension.dose: Initial 25 mg once daily; maintain 25–100 mg/day
Hypertension.comments: Useful in elderly or salt‑sensitive hypertension
Add when BP uncontrolled; watch for edema and flushing

Hypertension – Combination Therapy.dose: 5/5 mg once daily, titrate to 10/10 mg once daily
Hypertension – Combination Therapy.comments: Fixed‑dose combo improves adherence; for patients uncontrolled on monotherapy

Hypertension – Combination Therapy.dose: 50/12.5 mg once daily, titrate to 100/25 mg once daily
4/1.25 mg once daily, titrate as needed
Hypertension – Combination Therapy.comments: Alternate combination when CCB not tolerated

Hypertension – Combination Therapy.dose: 5/1.25/5 mg once daily; adjust to 10/2.5/10 mg
Hypertension – Combination Therapy.comments: For resistant hypertension; monitor electrolytes and renal function

Hypertensive Emergency/Urgency.dose: 0.1–0.2 mg orally then 0.1 mg every hour until goal or max 0.7 mg
Hypertensive Emergency/Urgency.comments: Short-term BP lowering while awaiting transfer

Hypertensive Emergency/Urgency.dose: 25 mg orally; may repeat every 1–2 hours
Hypertensive Emergency/Urgency.comments: Useful for oral management of severe hypertension

Hypertensive Emergency/Urgency.dose: 20 mg IV over 2 minutes; may repeat 40–80 mg every 10 min (max 300 mg)
Hypertensive Emergency/Urgency.comments: Used in emergency settings; not typically in office

Peripheral Arterial Disease.comments: Preferred for PAD; reduces cardiovascular events
Alternative antiplatelet if clopidogrel not used
For intermittent claudication; contraindicated in heart failure
All patients with PAD should receive statins

Stable Angina/CAD.dose: 50 mg orally twice daily; titrate to 100–200 mg/day
Stable Angina/CAD.comments: Reduces myocardial oxygen demand; avoid in bradycardia

Stable Angina/CAD.dose: 30–60 mg extended release once daily; max 120 mg/day
Stable Angina/CAD.comments: Provides symptomatic relief; ensure nitrate‑free interval
Use when beta blockers contraindicated or ineffective

Stable Angina/CAD.dose: 500 mg orally twice daily, up to 1,000 mg BID
Stable Angina/CAD.comments: Adjunct for chronic angina when other agents insufficient

Acne Vulgaris.dose: Apply thin layer once daily, increase to twice daily as tolerated
Acne Vulgaris.comments: Start at low concentration for sensitive skin

Acne Vulgaris.dose: Apply thin layer once daily at bedtime
Acne Vulgaris.comments: Combines retinoid and antimicrobial; may cause irritation

Acne Vulgaris.dose: 100 mg orally twice daily for 3–4 months
Acne Vulgaris.comments: Combine with topical benzoyl peroxide to reduce resistance

Acne Vulgaris.dose: 100 mg orally once or twice daily (1 mg/kg/day) for up to 12 weeks
Acne Vulgaris.comments: Alternative to doxycycline for moderate to severe inflammatory acne; limit duration to minimise adverse effects; monitor for liver and vestibular side effects

Acne Vulgaris.dose: 0.5–1 mg/kg/day orally in divided doses for 4–6 months
Acne Vulgaris.comments: Requires specialist initiation, pregnancy prevention program, monitoring of lipids and liver function

Cellulitis.dose: 500 mg four times daily for 5–7 days
300 mg three times daily for 5–7 days
Cellulitis.comments: Use when MRSA suspected or beta-lactam allergy

Cutaneous Warts (verruca).dose: After soaking, apply to wart once or twice daily for up to 12 weeks until wart resolved
Cutaneous Warts (verruca).comments: Soften skin before application; protect surrounding skin

Cutaneous Warts (verruca).dose: Freeze wart for 10–20 s; repeat every 2–3 weeks for up to 3–4 treatments
Cutaneous Warts (verruca).comments: Performed in clinic; first‑line along with salicylic acid

Eczema.dose: Apply liberally to entire body at least twice daily, especially after bathing; reapply as needed to maintain hydration
Eczema.comments: Alternative commercial emollients for eczema management; choose unscented, hypoallergenic products
Use for acute flares; limit duration

Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.dose: Apply thin layer to affected area once or twice daily; use ≤ 7 days
Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.comments: Use lowest effective potency; avoid face/genitals long‑term

Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.dose: Apply to affected area 1–2 times daily for up to 2 weeks
Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.comments: Stronger steroid for resistant flares; avoid prolonged use

Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.dose: Apply thin layer once daily; reassess if no improvement after 2 weeks
Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.comments: Use for short courses on trunk/limbs; avoid face/folds

Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.dose: Apply to affected areas once or twice daily for 1 week; maximum 4 weeks
Eczema/Atopic Dermatitis.comments: Reserved for severe, lichenified eczema; specialist supervision

Fungal Infections – Candida.comments: For cutaneous candidiasis and tinea versicolor

Fungal Infections – Tinea.dose: Apply once or twice daily for 1–4 weeks

Impetigo.dose: Apply to lesions three times daily for 5 days
500 mg four times daily for 7 days

Pityriasis Versicolor.dose: Shampoo: apply once daily for 5 days; Cream: apply once or twice daily for 2–3 weeks
Pityriasis Versicolor.comments: Advise patient to wash off shampoo after 5 minutes

Pityriasis Versicolor.dose: Apply lotion to affected area for 10 minutes daily for 7 days
400 mg orally single dose or 300 mg weekly for 2 weeks

Psoriasis.dose: Apply thin layer once or twice daily
15 mg orally or subcutaneously once weekly
Psoriasis.comments: For moderate to severe psoriasis; requires folic acid

Scalp Psoriasis.drug: Tar or salicylic acid shampoo; calcipotriol + betamethasone foam
Scalp Psoriasis.dose: Use medicated shampoo 2–3 times/week; apply vitamin D–steroid foam once daily
Apply ~7.5 mL to dry scalp once daily, leave 15 minutes, then rinse; max 4 weeks
Scalp Psoriasis.comments: Very potent steroid for scalp; avoid prolonged use

Tinea Corporis/Pedis/Cruris (Ringworm/At.dose: Apply to affected area twice daily for 2–4 weeks
Tinea Corporis/Pedis/Cruris (Ringworm/At.comments: Continue treatment for 1 week after lesions clear

Tinea Corporis/Pedis/Cruris (Ringworm/At.dose: Apply once or twice daily for 1–2 weeks
250 mg orally once daily for 2–6 weeks
150 mg orally once weekly for 2–4 weeks

Urticaria.comments: First-line therapy; may double dose if needed

Epistaxis.comments: Pinch nostrils for 10–15 minutes while leaning forward

Epistaxis.dose: 2 sprays in affected nostril every 12 hours
Apply to bleeding point for 10 seconds

Oral Ulcers/Dental Infections.dose: Apply to ulcer up to 4 times daily
500 mg orally three times daily for 5–7 days

Otitis Externa.dose: 4 drops into affected ear twice daily for 7 days

Otitis Media.dose: 500 mg orally three times daily for 5–7 days
Otitis Media.comments: First choice for acute otitis media

Otitis Media.dose: 875/125 mg orally twice daily for 5–7 days
Otitis Media.comments: Use when high-dose amoxicillin fails or risk factors present

Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.dose: 500 mg orally twice daily or 250 mg four times daily for 10 days
Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.comments: Treat only when Centor or FeverPAIN score ≥3; first-line narrow-spectrum antibiotic for confirmed or highly suspected streptococcal pharyngitis

Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.dose: 500 mg orally twice daily for 10 days
Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.comments: Second-line for recurrent infection or if broader spectrum needed; use for 10 days

Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.dose: 500 mg on day 1 then 250 mg days 2–5
Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.comments: Macrolide option for penicillin allergy; 5-day regimen; reserve for allergy or intolerances

Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.dose: 500/125 mg orally three times daily for 5–7 days
Pharyngitis/Tonsillitis.comments: Treat only when Centor or FeverPAIN score ≥3; first-line narrow-spectrum antibiotic for confirmed or highly suspected streptococcal pharyngitis

Sinusitis.dose: 500 mg orally three times daily for 5–7 days
100 mg orally twice daily for 5–7 days
500/125 mg orally three times daily for 5 days (alternative 875/125 mg twice daily)
Sinusitis.comments: For severe or high‑risk bacterial sinusitis or failure of first-line therapy; use cautiously due to increased gastrointestinal adverse effects and risk of resistance

B12/Folate Deficiency.dose: 1,000 mcg orally daily or IM monthly
1 mg orally daily for at least 4 months or until hematologic recovery; maintenance 0.4 mg daily in adults, 0.8 mg in pregnancy/lactation
B12/Folate Deficiency.comments: Therapeutic dose for folate deficiency; ensure B12 deficiency ruled out before treating

Hypercalcemia.dose: NS infusion (200–300 mL/h) plus furosemide 20–40 mg IV
4 units/kg IM or SC every 12 hours
Hypercalcemia.comments: Rapid but short-lived reduction in calcium
Longer-term control of hypercalcemia of malignancy

Hyperthyroidism.dose: 10–30 mg orally daily, divided every 8 hours initially
Hyperthyroidism.comments: Drug of choice for Graves disease

Hypocalcemia.dose: 500 mg elemental calcium orally three times daily
Hypocalcemia.comments: Acute symptomatic cases require IV calcium

Hypothyroidism.comments: Titrate based on TSH every 6–8 weeks

Iron Deficiency.comments: Take on empty stomach with vitamin C to enhance absorption

Iron Deficiency.dose: 200 mg IV every 1–2 weeks until total dose reached
Iron Deficiency.comments: Use when oral iron not tolerated or malabsorption present

Obesity.comments: Caloric restriction and increased physical activity

Obesity.dose: 120 mg orally three times daily with fat-containing meals
Obesity.comments: Decreases fat absorption; may cause oily stools
Promotes weight loss and improves glycemic control
Reduces appetite; monitor BP and mood

Osteoporosis.comments: Ensure adequate intake for bone health
First-line pharmacologic therapy; take on empty stomach
For high fracture risk or intolerance to bisphosphonates
Reserved for severe osteoporosis or multiple fractures

PCOS.comments: Regulates menses and lowers androgen levels

PCOS.dose: 500 mg twice daily; titrate to 850–1,000 mg BID

Prediabetes.comments: Weight loss and increased physical activity

Type 1 Diabetes.dose: Basal ~0.2 units/kg/day; bolus 0.05–0.1 units/kg per meal
Type 1 Diabetes.comments: Mainstay therapy; adjust per carbohydrate intake

Type 1 Diabetes.dose: 15 mcg subcutaneously before meals; titrate to 60 mcg
Type 1 Diabetes.comments: Use in adults to reduce postprandial glucose

Type 2 Diabetes.dose: Start 500 mg once or twice daily; titrate to 1,000 mg twice daily
Type 2 Diabetes.comments: First-line pharmacologic agent; avoid if eGFR <30 mL/min

Type 2 Diabetes.dose: 10 units subcutaneously at bedtime; titrate to fasting glucose target

Type 2 Diabetes – Combination Therapy.dose: Metformin 500 mg + sitagliptin 50 mg twice daily or 100 mg once daily with meals
Type 2 Diabetes – Combination Therapy.comments: Use when glycemic control not achieved with metformin alone

Type 2 Diabetes – Combination Therapy.dose: Empagliflozin 10 mg once daily with metformin; increase to 25 mg as needed

Type 2 Diabetes – Combination Therapy.comments: GLP‑1 agonist provides weight loss and cardiovascular benefits

Type 2 Diabetes – Combination Therapy.dose: Initiate insulin glargine 10 units subcutaneously at bedtime; titrate by 2 units every 3 days to achieve fasting glucose target
Type 2 Diabetes – Combination Therapy.comments: Consider when oral agents/GLP‑1 therapy insufficient

Anal Fissure.drug: High‑fiber diet, stool softeners, sitz baths
Anal Fissure.dose: Increase fiber; docusate 100 mg BID; warm baths 2–3 times/day
Apply 1 inch of ointment to anal canal every 12 h
Anal Fissure.comments: Relaxes internal sphincter to promote healing

Anal Fissure.dose: Apply small amount to anal canal 2–3 times daily
Anal Fissure.comments: Used when nitroglycerin not tolerated or ineffective
For chronic fissures refractory to medical therapy

Constipation.dose: 1 tablespoon (~3.4 g) in 8 oz water 1–3 times daily
17.2 mg (2 tablets) once daily at bedtime
Constipation.comments: For short-term use when other laxatives ineffective
Reduces stool hardness; often combined with stimulant

Diarrhea.dose: Reconstitute per instructions and sip frequently
4 mg initially then 2 mg after each loose stool (max 16 mg/day)
Diarrhea.comments: Symptomatic relief of acute noninfectious diarrhea

Diarrhea.dose: 524 mg (30 mL or 2 tablets) every 30–60 minutes (max 8 doses/day)
Diarrhea.comments: Useful for traveler’s diarrhea and dyspepsia

Diarrhea.dose: 500 mg orally twice daily for 3 days
Diarrhea.comments: Empiric therapy for severe traveler’s diarrhea

Fatty Liver/Hepatitis.comments: Cornerstone therapy for nonalcoholic fatty liver disease
First-line agent for chronic hepatitis B

GERD/Dyspepsia.dose: 20 mg orally once daily for 4–8 weeks

GERD/Dyspepsia.comments: Use when PPIs contraindicated or for breakthrough at night

Gallstones.dose: 400 mg every 6 hours as needed for pain

Gallstones.comments: Dissolves cholesterol gallstones in select patients

Hemorrhoids (Piles).drug: Fiber supplements, stool softeners (e.g., psyllium, docusate)
Hemorrhoids (Piles).dose: Increase dietary fiber; take docusate 100 mg orally BID; warm sitz baths

Hemorrhoids (Piles).drug: Hydrocortisone 1% rectal cream or suppository; topical lidocaine
Hemorrhoids (Piles).dose: Apply cream morning, night and after bowel movement for up to 7 days
Hemorrhoids (Piles).comments: Short course for symptomatic relief; avoid prolonged steroid use

Hemorrhoids (Piles).drug: Rubber band ligation, sclerotherapy or infrared coagulation
Hemorrhoids (Piles).dose: Performed in clinic; typically single session with repeat as needed

Hemorrhoids (Piles).comments: Reserved for severe or prolapsed hemorrhoids

Hemorrhoids/Fissures.comments: Improve stool consistency and reduce irritation

Hemorrhoids/Fissures.dose: Apply to affected area twice daily
Apply to anal canal every 12 hours
Hemorrhoids/Fissures.comments: For chronic anal fissures to reduce sphincter spasm

IBS.dose: 2 mg after each loose stool (max 16 mg/day)

Helicobacter pylori infection.dose: PPI standard dose BID + Amoxicillin 1 g BID + Clarithromycin 500 mg BID + Metronidazole 500 mg BID for 14 days
Helicobacter pylori infection.comments: First-line clarithromycin-based quadruple regimen (PAMC/CLAMET); avoid if prior macrolide exposure; ensure adherence and confirm eradication

Helicobacter pylori infection.comments: Alternative first-line bismuth quadruple therapy (PBMT); use when clarithromycin resistance suspected or previous macrolide exposure; ensure adherence

Helicobacter pylori infection.dose: PPI BID + Amoxicillin 1 g BID + Levofloxacin 500 mg once daily for 14 days

Helicobacter pylori infection.dose: PPI BID + Amoxicillin 1 g BID + Rifabutin 150 mg BID for 10 days
Helicobacter pylori infection.comments: Fourth-line therapy (rifabutin-based) reserved for multiple treatment failures; risk of myelotoxicity; consult gastroenterologist

Anogenital Warts.dose: Apply thin film at bedtime 3 times per week; wash off after 6–10 h; continue until warts clear or up to 16 weeks
Apply twice daily for 3 days, then no therapy for 4 days; may repeat up to 4 cycles; total area ≤10 cm²/day
Apply thin layer 3 times daily until clearance or up to 16 weeks
Anogenital Warts.comments: Patient‑applied; avoid occlusive dressings and sexual contact

Anogenital Warts.dose: Applied by clinician every 1–3 weeks until clearance

Chlamydia trachomatis Infection.dose: 100 mg orally twice daily for 7 days
Chlamydia trachomatis Infection.comments: Avoid in pregnancy; treat partners concurrently

Chlamydia trachomatis Infection.dose: 1 g orally as a single dose
Chlamydia trachomatis Infection.comments: Single‑dose alternative if adherence is a concern

Chlamydia trachomatis Infection.dose: 500 mg orally once daily for 7 days

Genital Herpes (HSV).dose: 400 mg orally three times daily for 7–10 days (initial episode)
Genital Herpes (HSV).comments: Start at first sign of lesions; hydration important

Genital Herpes (HSV).dose: 1 g orally twice daily for 7–10 days (initial episode)

Gonorrhea (uncomplicated).comments: Also treat empirically for chlamydia if not excluded

Gonorrhea (uncomplicated).drug: Ceftriaxone 500 mg IM plus azithromycin 1 g orally
Gonorrhea (uncomplicated).dose: Single 500 mg IM dose plus azithromycin 1 g orally
Gonorrhea (uncomplicated).comments: Use where chlamydia coinfection likely or resistance patterns dictate

Trichomoniasis.dose: 2 g orally as a single dose
Trichomoniasis.comments: Single dose therapy; treat sexual partners

Trichomoniasis.dose: 500 mg orally twice daily for 7 days
Trichomoniasis.comments: Alternative regimen; often used in women

Dementia.dose: 5 mg nightly; titrate to 10 mg after 4–6 weeks
Dementia.comments: Improves cognition in mild to moderate Alzheimer disease

Dementia.dose: 5 mg daily; increase to 10 mg twice daily

End-of-Life Care.dose: 2.5–5 mg orally or subcutaneously every 4 hours

End-of-Life Care.dose: 0.5–1 mg sublingually or subcutaneously every 4–6 hours
End-of-Life Care.comments: Relieves anxiety and agitation in terminal illness

End-of-Life Care.dose: 0.5–1 mg orally or subcutaneously every 4 hours

Falls Prevention.dose: Vitamin D 800 IU and calcium 1,200 mg daily
Falls Prevention.comments: Improve bone strength and reduce fall risk

Frailty.comments: Supports muscle function and bone health

Abnormal Uterine Bleeding.dose: 500 mg initially then 250 mg every 6–8 hours during menses
1 g three times daily for up to 5 days
1 tablet daily for 3 weeks then placebo for 1 week

Contraception Counselling.dose: 0.35 mg daily at same time each day
52 mg device inserted intrauterine; effective up to 5 years
68 mg subdermal implant effective for 3 years
Contraception Counselling.comments: Long-acting contraception; may cause irregular bleeding

Dysmenorrhea.dose: 400 mg every 6 hours at onset of menses

Dysmenorrhea.comments: Suppresses ovulation and reduces menstrual pain
Alternative for women who cannot take estrogen

Emergency Contraception.comments: Most effective if taken as soon as possible
More effective later in the 5-day window; prescription only

Emergency Contraception.dose: Inserted within 5 days of unprotected intercourse
Emergency Contraception.comments: Provides ongoing contraception for up to 10 years

Menopause Symptoms.comments: Alleviates vasomotor symptoms; use lowest effective dose

Menopause Symptoms.dose: 300 mg at bedtime; may increase to 900 mg/day
Menopause Symptoms.comments: Improves vasomotor symptoms in some women

STI – Gonorrhea.comments: Combine with azithromycin for dual coverage
Given with ceftriaxone to cover potential chlamydial co-infection

Vaginitis (Bacterial).comments: First-line treatment for bacterial vaginosis: metronidazole 500 mg PO BID for 7 days (or 2 g single dose); treat symptomatic patients; partner treatment not usually required; avoid alcohol

Vaginitis (Candidiasis).comments: First-line treatment for vulvovaginal candidiasis: oral fluconazole 150 mg single dose; consider topical azoles for pregnant or recurrent cases

Vaginitis (Trichomoniasis).comments: First-line therapy: metronidazole 2 g single dose or 500 mg BID for 7 days; treat sexual partners concurrently; avoid alcohol

B12 Deficiency.dose: 1,000 mcg IM monthly or 1,000–2,000 mcg orally daily

Polycythemia Vera.comments: For high-risk patients to control blood counts

Thrombocytopenia (ITP).comments: Use when rapid platelet rise required

Lyme Disease (early localized).dose: 500 mg three times daily for 14 days
Lyme Disease (early localized).comments: Alternative in pregnancy or children under 8

Meningitis Triage.dose: 0.15 mg/kg IV every 6 hours for 4 days

Indicated for influenza within 48 h of symptom onset

Cognitive Decline/Dementia.dose: 5 mg at bedtime; increase to 10 mg after 4–6 weeks

Headache/Migraine.dose: 40 mg twice daily; titrate to 160–240 mg/day

Neuropathy (Peripheral).dose: 300 mg at bedtime; titrate to 300 mg three times daily
Neuropathy (Peripheral).comments: For diabetic and postherpetic neuropathic pain

Parkinsonism.dose: 0.125 mg three times daily; titrate to 1.5 mg TID

Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy.dose: 100 mg twice daily; titrate to 200–400 mg BID

Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy.dose: 30 mg extended-release daily; titrate to 60–90 mg/day
250 mg two to three times daily
Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy.comments: Historic first-line; now less commonly used

Nausea/Vomiting in Pregnancy.dose: 10 mg/10 mg – 2 tablets at bedtime; up to 4/day
Nausea/Vomiting in Pregnancy.comments: First-line pharmacologic therapy for morning sickness
Second-line for hyperemesis gravidarum; consider risk–benefit

Postpartum Care.comments: Preferred analgesic for postpartum uterine cramping

ADHD.dose: Start 5 mg twice daily; increase by 5–10 mg weekly to max 60 mg/day

ADHD.comments: Alternative when stimulants contraindicated or not tolerated

ADHD.dose: 0.05 mg at bedtime; titrate to 0.3 mg/day
ADHD.comments: Adjunct therapy for hyperactivity and impulsivity

Acne.dose: Apply once daily, increase to twice daily as tolerated

Acne.comments: Use with benzoyl peroxide to reduce resistance
For moderate to severe inflammatory acne (≥8 yrs)

Asthma/Wheeze.dose: 100 mcg (1 puff) per 10 kg up to 4 puffs every 4 hours PRN
50 mcg twice daily; adjust to control

Bronchiolitis.dose: 2 puffs via MDI with spacer every 4 hours
Bronchiolitis.comments: May provide transient relief in select patients

Eczema.dose: Apply to entire body at least twice daily and after bathing; reapply frequently
Eczema.comments: Alternative emollients appropriate for children; avoid scented products

Eczema.dose: Apply thin layer twice daily for up to 7 days
Eczema.comments: Use low-potency steroid for face and folds

Enuresis.dose: 0.2 mg at bedtime; increase to 0.6 mg
Enuresis.comments: Effective but relapse common after discontinuation
Use only when other treatments fail; monitor for cardiotoxicity

Fever.comments: First-line for fever and pain in children
Use in children older than 6 months

Anxiety Disorders.comments: Also effective for comorbid pain syndromes

Anxiety Disorders.dose: 2–5 mg two to four times daily
Anxiety Disorders.comments: Short-term use for acute anxiety; risk of dependence

Anxiety Disorders.dose: 10 mg twice daily; titrate to 60 mg/day
Anxiety Disorders.comments: Non-sedating alternative; takes weeks to work

Bipolar Disorder (suspected).dose: 300 mg two to three times daily; adjust to serum levels 0.6–1.2 mEq/L
250 mg three times daily; titrate to therapeutic levels
Bipolar Disorder (suspected).comments: Alternative to lithium; monitor liver function
Preferred for bipolar depression; slow titration to reduce rash

Depression.dose: 50 mg once daily; may increase to 200 mg/day
75 mg once daily; titrate to 225 mg/day
150 mg once daily in morning; may increase to 300 mg/day
Depression.comments: Stimulating option; avoid in seizure disorder

Depression.dose: 25–50 mg at bedtime; titrate to 150–300 mg/day
Depression.comments: For treatment-resistant depression; anticholinergic side effects

Insomnia.comments: Establish regular sleep schedule; avoid caffeine and screens
Useful for jet lag and delayed sleep phase
Limit to short-term use; risk of dependence
For patients with comorbid depression and insomnia

PTSD.comments: First-line pharmacologic treatment; combine with therapy

PTSD.dose: 1 mg at bedtime; titrate to 5–15 mg
PTSD.comments: For PTSD-related nightmares and sleep disturbance

PTSD.dose: 25 mg at bedtime; titrate to 150–300 mg
PTSD.comments: Adjunct for severe symptoms or psychotic features

Panic Disorder.dose: 0.25 mg twice daily; titrate to 1–2 mg/day

Substance Use Disorder – Alcohol.comments: Reduces cravings and heavy drinking days
Aversive therapy; patient must be highly motivated

Substance Use Disorder – Opioids.dose: 2 mg/0.5 mg sublingually; titrate to 8–16 mg/2–4 mg daily
Substance Use Disorder – Opioids.comments: First-line for opioid use disorder; office-based therapy
For highly motivated patients after detoxification

Suicidality.comments: Start after risk assessment; monitor closely

Chronic Kidney Disease.dose: 2.5 mg once daily; titrate as tolerated
50 mg once daily; titrate to 100 mg/day

Chronic Kidney Disease.comments: Offers kidney and cardiovascular protection in CKD
For volume control in advanced CKD

Hyperkalemia.comments: Promotes potassium excretion; used in non-emergent cases

Hyperkalemia.dose: 20–40 mg IV or orally once; titrate
Hyperkalemia.comments: Increases renal K+ excretion; requires adequate volume status

Hyperkalemia.dose: 10 units IV insulin with 25 g dextrose IV

Hyponatremia.dose: 0.5–2 mL/kg/h IV; monitor serum sodium closely

Nephrolithiasis.comments: NSAID provides pain relief and reduces ureteric spasm
Facilitates passage of distal ureteric stones

Nephrolithiasis.dose: 10–20 mEq orally two to three times daily

Acute Bronchitis.dose: 2 puffs (100 mcg each) via MDI every 4–6 hours PRN
Acute Bronchitis.comments: Use only if wheeze or bronchospasm

Acute Bronchitis.dose: 500 mg orally on day 1 then 250 mg daily on days 2–5
Acute Bronchitis.comments: Reserved for pertussis or atypical infection

Asthma.dose: 2 puffs (100 mcg each) inhaled as needed, up to every 4 hours

Asthma.comments: Controller therapy; adjust dose to control symptoms
Add-on when inhaled corticosteroid alone insufficient

Asthma.dose: 10 mg orally once daily in adults
Asthma.comments: Alternative or add-on for mild persistent asthma

Asthma.dose: Specialist initiation and dosing per agent; subcutaneous injection every 2–8 weeks
Asthma.comments: For severe eosinophilic or allergic asthma unresponsive to maximal inhaler therapy; specialist use only

Asthma.dose: Short course of 0.5–1 mg/kg/day orally (max 50 mg/day) for 3–5 days for exacerbations
Asthma.comments: Use for acute exacerbations when rescue inhalers and controllers insufficient; taper not usually needed for short courses

COPD.comments: Improves lung function and reduces exacerbations
For severe COPD with chronic bronchitis and frequent exacerbations
For patients with severe COPD and frequent exacerbations despite dual therapy

Community-Acquired Pneumonia.dose: 500 mg orally every 8 hours or 1 g twice daily for 5–7 days
Community-Acquired Pneumonia.comments: First-line for outpatient CAP in healthy adults

Community-Acquired Pneumonia.dose: 100 mg orally twice daily for 5–7 days
500 mg on day 1 then 250 mg days 2–5
750 mg orally once daily for 5 days
Community-Acquired Pneumonia.comments: Reserve for comorbidities or recent antibiotic use

Community-Acquired Pneumonia.dose: 500/125 mg orally three times daily for 5–7 days (or 875/125 mg twice daily)
Community-Acquired Pneumonia.comments: For moderate-severity CAP (CRB‑65 score 1–2) or after first-line failure; treat 5–7 days; evaluate need for hospital referral; caution gastrointestinal adverse effects and C. difficile infection

Croup (Pediatrics).comments: Improves symptoms and reduces ED visits

Croup (Pediatrics).dose: 0.5 mL of 2.25% solution diluted with saline; may repeat
Croup (Pediatrics).comments: For moderate to severe croup to relieve airway obstruction

Obstructive Sleep Apnea.dose: Use nightly; pressure titrated during sleep study, typical 4–20 cm H2O
Obstructive Sleep Apnea.comments: First‑line therapy for moderate to severe OSA

Obstructive Sleep Apnea.dose: Custom oral device worn nightly to advance the mandible and open airway
Obstructive Sleep Apnea.comments: Alternative for mild OSA or CPAP‑intolerant patients; fitted by dentist

Obstructive Sleep Apnea.dose: Use nightly; delivers two pressures (IPAP/EPAP) with typical range 4–30 cm H2O
Obstructive Sleep Apnea.comments: For patients who cannot tolerate CPAP or have central sleep apnea

Obstructive Sleep Apnea.drug: Upper airway surgery or hypoglossal nerve stimulation
Obstructive Sleep Apnea.comments: Surgical interventions (UPPP, maxillomandibular advancement) or implanted neurostimulators for severe CPAP‑refractory OSA

URTI/Influenza-like.comments: Symptomatic relief of fever and pain
Relieves nasal congestion; avoid in hypertension

Fibromyalgia.comments: Helps with pain and mood symptoms

Rheumatoid Arthritis (suspected).comments: Symptom relief; does not alter disease progression
Short-term control until DMARDs take effect
Anchor drug; folic acid supplementation required

Erectile Dysfunction.dose: 50 mg one hour before intercourse (25–100 mg range)
Erectile Dysfunction.comments: Avoid with nitrates; adjust dose for hepatic/renal impairment

Erectile Dysfunction.dose: 10 mg prior to intercourse or 5 mg once daily
Erectile Dysfunction.comments: Longer half-life; may be used daily
For severe cases; counsel on administration

LUTS/BPH.comments: Improves urinary flow; may cause dizziness
Reduces prostate volume; takes months to see effect
For moderate to severe symptoms with enlarged prostate
Useful in men with concomitant ED

Prostatitis (acute bacterial).comments: Alternative therapy; monitor for adverse effects

Pyelonephritis.comments: Alternative regimen; adjust for kidney function

Pyelonephritis.dose: 1 g IV/IM once then transition to oral agent
Pyelonephritis.comments: Initial dose for severe infection or pregnancy

Testicular Pain (Epididymitis).comments: Covers N. gonorrhoeae; used with doxycycline

Urinary Tract Infection (simple).comments: Alternative first-line agent; avoid if resistance >20%

Urinary Tract Infection (simple).dose: 3 g orally as a single dose
Urinary Tract Infection (simple).comments: Convenient one-dose therapy; safe in pregnancy

Urinary Tract Infection (simple).dose: 500 mg orally twice daily for 3 days
Urinary Tract Infection (simple).comments: Reserve for complicated infections due to resistance concerns
